title: Cosmic Time Machine Reveals Ancient Galaxy
story: Peering deep into space, scientists made an incredible discovery in 2015. They found EGS-zs8-1, the oldest and most distant galaxy ever seen! This cosmic fossil formed when the universe was very young. Powerful telescopes acted like time machines, letting researchers glimpse light that traveled for billions of years to reach Earth. The announcement came on May 5, opening a window into the early days of our universe.
activity_question: What did scientists discover in 2015?
activity_choices: The oldest and most distant galaxy
A new planet in our solar system
A cure for the common cold
activity_answer: The oldest and most distant galaxy
follow_up_question: Why are powerful telescopes like time machines when looking at distant galaxies?

title: Mets Triumph in Final Mayor's Trophy Game
story: Baseball fans cheered as the New York Mets clinched victory in a special match. On May 5, 1981, they faced off against their crosstown rivals, the New York Yankees, in the last-ever Mayor's Trophy Game. This friendly competition had been a beloved tradition for years. title: Planets Line Up for Cosmic Dance
story: The sky put on a dazzling show in 2000! On May 5, stargazers witnessed an incredible sight as five planets and the moon lined up with the sun. Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, and Saturn joined our moon in a rare cosmic dance. This special arrangement, called a conjunction, doesn't happen often. title: Eugene Bullard: Trailblazing Pilot Takes Flight
story: Eugene Bullard soared into history on May 5, 1917. This brave aviator earned his pilot's license from the Aéro-Club de France, becoming the first African-American military pilot. His determination and skill paved the way for future generations of diverse pilots to spread their wings and chase their dreams.

title: Voyager 1's Close Encounter with Jupiter

This flyby marked a major milestone in our exploration of the outer solar system.

story: History was made in Atlanta, Georgia when Eugene A. Marino became the first African American Catholic archbishop. Installed on May 5, 1988, Archbishop Marino's appointment marked a significant step towards diversity in the church's leadership. His groundbreaking role inspired many and opened doors for future generations of African American clergy.
activity_question: Where was Eugene A. Marino installed as the first African American Catholic archbishop?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for leadership roles to reflect diverse communities?

title: Rocket Blasts Off with Satellite Swarm
story: A powerful Delta 2 rocket roared to life on May 5, 1997. Its mission? To carry the first batch of Iridium satellites into orbit. These tiny spacecraft were part of a plan to create a network for global phone calls. The launch was a success, marking the start of a new era in space-based communication.
activity_question: What type of rocket was used for this launch?
bonus_fact: Iridium satellites are named after the chemical element iridium.

title: Japan's Nuclear Power Pause
story: Japan flipped the switch on a big change in 2012. For the first time in over 40 years, the country powered down all its nuclear reactors on May 5. This meant Japan had to find new ways to keep the lights on. People worked hard to save energy and use other power sources like wind and solar.
activity_question: What did Japan do for the first time in over 40 years?
activity_choices: Powered down all its nuclear reactors
activity_answer: Powered down all its nuclear reactors
follow_up_question: Can you think of ways to save energy at home?
